Fungal infection: A fungal infection caused by fungi on skin, nails or mucous membranes. Usually not dangerous but spreads easily.
Allergy: An immune system reaction to foreign substances. Can cause sneezing, rash, or breathing difficulty.
GERD: Gastroesophageal reflux disease — stomach acid flows back into food pipe causing heartburn and chest discomfort.
Diabetes: A chronic condition where the body cannot properly regulate blood sugar levels.
Hypertension: High blood pressure — the force of blood pushing against artery walls is consistently too high.
Malaria: A life-threatening disease spread by mosquito bites, causing high fever and chills.
Dengue: A mosquito-borne viral infection causing high fever, rash, and severe joint pain.
Typhoid: A bacterial infection spread through contaminated food or water causing prolonged fever.
Common Cold: A viral infection of the nose and throat. Usually harmless but uncomfortable.
Pneumonia: Infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid.
Tuberculosis: A bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs. Spreads through the air.
Chicken pox: A highly contagious viral infection causing an itchy blister-like rash on the skin.
Jaundice: Yellowing of the skin and eyes caused by excess bilirubin in the blood.
Migraine: A headache disorder causing severe throbbing pain, often with nausea and light sensitivity.
Heart attack: Occurs when blood flow to part of the heart is blocked. A medical emergency.
Urinary tract infection: An infection in any part of the urinary system — kidneys, bladder, or urethra.
Acne: A skin condition when hair follicles plug with oil and dead skin cells.
Arthritis: Inflammation of joints causing pain and stiffness that typically worsens with age.
Gastroenteritis: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically from viral or bacterial infection.
Bronchial Asthma: A condition in which airways narrow and swell, causing breathing difficulty.
Hypothyroidism: The thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormone, slowing body functions.
Hyperthyroidism: The thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, speeding body functions.
Psoriasis: A skin disease causing red, itchy scaly patches, especially on knees, elbows and scalp.
Hepatitis A: A liver infection caused by the hepatitis A virus, usually spread through contaminated food.
Hepatitis B: A liver infection caused by the hepatitis B virus. Can become chronic.
